mainmatter.p: We expand the dependent sum { \textstyle \sum _{ g \colon X \to Y }} g \sim f as a flat record telescope:
mainmatter.tex: \begin {bmatrix*}[l] { \mathopen {} \left \lvert g \right \rvert \mathclose {}} & \colon & { \mathopen {} \left \lvert X \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \to { \mathopen {} \left \lvert Y \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \\ \alpha _g & \colon & { \mathopen {} \left \lvert g \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \circ \alpha _X \sim \alpha _Y \\ { \mathopen {} \left \lvert H \right \rvert \mathclose {}} & \colon & { \mathopen {} \left \lvert f \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \sim { \mathopen {} \left \lvert g \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \\ \alpha _H & \colon & { \textstyle \prod _{ a:A }} { \mathopen {} \left \lvert H \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \alpha _X a \cdot \alpha _g a = \alpha _f a \end {bmatrix*}

\begin {bmatrix*}[l] { \mathopen {} \left \lvert g \right \rvert \mathclose {}} & \colon & { \mathopen {} \left \lvert X \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \to { \mathopen {} \left \lvert Y \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \\ { \mathopen {} \left \lvert H \right \rvert \mathclose {}} & \colon & { \mathopen {} \left \lvert f \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \sim { \mathopen {} \left \lvert g \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \\ \alpha _g & \colon & { \mathopen {} \left \lvert g \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \circ \alpha _X \sim \alpha _Y \\ \alpha _H & \colon & { \textstyle \prod _{ a:A }} { \mathopen {} \left \lvert H \right \rvert \mathclose {}} \alpha _X a \cdot \alpha _g a = \alpha _f a \end {bmatrix*}
mainmatter.p: By homotopy induction (function extensionality), the first two fields contract to a point with substitution { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \lvert g \right \rvert \mathclose {}} : \equiv { \mathopen {} \left \lvert f \right \rvert \mathclose {}} ; { \mathopen {} \left \lvert H \right \rvert \mathclose {}} : \equiv \lambda \_ . \mathsf { refl } \right ] \mathclose {}}. What remains of our telescope is the following:
Zooming into the \alpha _H component, we simplify it and then zoom back out:

By dependent homotopy induction (also function extensionality), the remaining data collapse to a point with substitution { \mathopen {} \left [ \alpha _g : \equiv \alpha _f; \alpha _H : \equiv \lambda \_ . \mathsf { refl } \right ] \mathclose {}}. The remaining telescope is empty, so we are done.

frontmatter.title: Structure identity principle for co-slice morphisms

mainmatter.p: Let A be a type and let { X } \xrightarrow {{ f }}{ Y } be a homomorphism of A-algebras. Assuming function extensionality, the type { \textstyle \sum _{ g \colon X \to Y }} g \sim f of homomorphisms homotopic to f is contractible.
